commit.message:
fix(auth): 429 rate limit backoff + admin_settings RLS simplification

1. Supabase client now uses custom fetch with automatic retry + exponential
   backoff for 429 responses. Prevents the auth death spiral where rate-limited
   token refresh fires SIGNED_OUT → clears session → retry → 429 again.

2. Simplified admin_settings RLS from has_role() dependency to simple
   user_id = auth.uid(). The has_role() check created a circular dependency
   with user_roles RLS, causing 42501 on INSERT for super_admin users.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

eng: fix admin_settings RLS INSERT policy (42501)

Split single FOR ALL policy into separate SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE
policies. The combined policy was causing 42501 insufficient_privilege
on INSERT for super_admin users. Migration deployed via API.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

eng: reduce console noise in AuthContext, AdminModeContext, useBookings

- Remove console.log debug statements from AuthContext (auto-create, tenant switch)
- Downgrade console.warn to comments for expected session retry scenario
- Silence AdminModeContext console.error for expected RLS/permission failures
- Replace console.error in fire-and-forget trigger-workflow/busy_blocks catches
  with silent catch handlers (main operation already succeeded)
- Remove redundant cancelBooking console.error (onError toast handles user-facing)
- Update 3 tests to match new patterns

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Smooth out GMV trend line chart

The viewBox was 100 units wide with preserveAspectRatio="none", causing
the stroke to appear thicker at steep angles despite non-scaling-stroke.
Increased viewBox to 500x proportional height so the coordinate space
better matches rendered pixels, eliminating visible thickness variation.
Also unique gradient IDs per chart instance to avoid conflicts.

* feat: allow super admins to invite team members

- Frontend: treat app_role='admin' as team owner in TeamsPage so they
  can see the Settings tab and use the invite UI
- Backend: invite-member edge function now checks caller's app_role
  and allows admins to bypass the team.created_by ownership check

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: check team owner's plan (not caller's) when admin sends invite

The plan/billing check used userId (the caller) to look up the plan,
but when a super admin sends the invite, their profile has no paid plan.
Now uses team.created_by to check the actual team owner's plan.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: show actual error message in invite-member for debugging

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: show actual error on invite failure instead of generic message

Catch network/CORS errors separately, handle non-JSON responses,
and show HTTP status code when error body is empty.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: disable gateway JWT verification for invite-member edge function

The Supabase gateway was rejecting requests with 401 before our
function code ran, returning non-JSON without CORS headers. Our
function already handles JWT verification internally (lines 40-57),
so gateway verification is redundant and prevents proper error responses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: super admins bypass plan/billing and member limit checks for invites

Super admins (app_role='admin') now skip all plan, billing, and member
count checks when sending invites. Previously failed because admin
plan is not in PLAN_MAX_MEMBERS map.

- Add Dashboard button to desktop nav bar before Teams
- Fix coaching note replies not showing immediately for team members
  (was reading from stale AuthContext profile instead of updated members state)
- Hide invite code from non-admin/non-owner team members
- Allow all members to view group dashboard (roster + challenges only)
- Hide listings and standups from regular members in group dashboard

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

All functions already handle auth internally via getUser(). The gateway
JWT check was causing 401s after the domain change to realtygrind.co.

AuthContext:
- Debounce SIGNED_OUT event by 500ms to prevent transient profile

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: bypass size/quality guard in apple_compat mode for animated image→HEVC

In apple_compat mode, the goal is Apple device playability, not file size
reduction. Animated AVIF (and other non-native formats) are not playable
in Apple Photos regardless of size, so a larger HEVC/GIF output is always
preferable to copying the non-playable original.

- vid_hevc/src/animated_image.rs:
  - convert_to_hevc_mp4_matched(): add `size_guard_active = !apple_compat`
    to skip the "output larger than input → copy original" guard
  - Add `quality_or_compat_ok = quality_passed || (apple_compat && SSIM≥0.90)`
    to also bypass the quality/stream-size gate that was discarding the HEVC
    temp file and falling back to copying the non-playable source
  - convert_to_gif_apple_compat(): same size_guard_active fix

- CHANGELOG.md: document the apple_compat guard bypass fixes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add search_path security for database functions

Created migration 008 to set search_path for functions:
- delete_user_account(uuid): SET search_path = public
- update_updated_at_column(): SET search_path = public

This is a security best practice to prevent search_path injection
attacks on SECURITY DEFINER functions.

Run this migration in Supabase SQL Editor:
supabase/migrations/008_set_function_search_path.sql

commit.message:
feat(credentials): extract reusable credentials module (Phase A) (#7)

* feat(credentials): extract reusable credentials module (Phase A)

- Add `Provider` type ('anthropic' | 'openai' | 'ollama') and `Credentials` type alias
- Add `ensureCredentials(opts?)` — interactive wizard that checks for existing creds or prompts user
- Add `validateCredentials(creds)` — delegates to existing `validateConnection`
- Add `getProviderClient(creds)` — returns typed client with `call()` for AI requests
- Make credential paths lazy (computed via `homedir()` at call time) for testability
- Update `saveCredentials` to accept a `Credentials` object instead of positional args
- Make `loadCredentials` provider arg optional — returns first found when unspecified
- Refactor `commands/connect.ts` to delegate wizard logic to `ensureCredentials({ force: true })`
- Add `engine/credentials.test.ts` with 13 tests covering load/save/round-trip
- Update package.json test script to run credentials tests alongside database tests

* chore: mark 010a-refactor-credentials as done (PR #7)

fix(talos): local NTP and disable ResourceHealthStatus feature gate

- Add UDM-SE gateway (192.168.30.1) as primary NTP server with
  time.cloudflare.com as fallback; prefer local over internet for
  reliability during outages
- Comment out ResourceHealthStatus in kubelet featureGates and apiServer
  feature-gates; K8s 1.35 kubelet calls v1alpha1.DRAResourceHealth on
  all DRA plugins but intel-gpu-resource-driver v0.9.1 does not
  implement it, causing ~5s spam on k8s-2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v10.1.2109: Fix materials tab + farmerData SyntaxError

Root cause: Jinja2 autoescaping converted '""' to '""' in the
amasUser field of window.farmerData, causing "Unexpected token '&'"
SyntaxError. This broke window.farmerData entirely, so farmer_id
defaulted to 1, and /farmer/1/materials-inventory returned 403.

Fixes:
1. Add |safe to all '""' fallbacks in farmerData block
2. Switch materials fetch to /farmer/api/materials (session-auth, no
   farmer_id in URL) as defense-in-depth
3. Add is_active filter and logging to /farmer/api/materials endpoint

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fit-any-window: fixed 1920x1080 canvas with CSS scale transform

- Slides designed at 1920x1080 with fixed large fonts (48px title, 24px body)
- JS calculates scale factor on resize: min(viewportW/1920, viewportH/1080)
- Content always fits any window, maintaining 16:9 proportions
- Dark letterbox on non-16:9 viewports for professional presentation look
- No more clamp/vmin — single design, one scale

commit.message:
Add Vercel Edge Middleware for social media OG meta tags

Social crawlers (WhatsApp, Twitter, Facebook, Telegram, etc.) don't
execute JavaScript so they can't read SPA client-side meta tags.
Middleware detects social bot user-agents and returns minimal HTML
with correct OG/Twitter meta per route:

- / → crypto research & scoring meta
- /sentiment → market sentiment analysis meta
- /changelog → score updates & platform history meta
- /coin/:ticker → dynamic coin-specific meta

Normal browsers and search engine bots pass through to the SPA.

Scale event caps by universe size (25% coverage target)

from TOTAL_SYSTEMS. Base values (tuned for 600 systems) are scaled

caps ~17x higher.

Processor and simulator now use scaled caps instead of hardcoded constants.

commit.message:
feat: per-database Fly app isolation + dedicated API app + TLS

* feat(api): add Fly app lifecycle CRUD helpers (US-001)

New flyApps module with createApp, deleteApp, getApp functions for
managing per-database Fly.io apps. Includes FlyAppError class with
status codes and 14 unit tests covering success and error paths
(409 duplicate, 404 not-found, non-JSON responses, missing token).

Also adds vitest as test framework for the API package.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(infra): migrate API to dedicated snoopgres-api Fly app (US-003)

Separate the API from the old shared snoopgres-db-engine Fly app into
its own snoopgres-api app for clean API/database isolation.

- fly.toml: change app name from snoopgres-db-engine to snoopgres-api
- CLI api-client: update default URL to https://snoopgres-api.fly.dev
- SDK client: update default base URL to https://snoopgres-api.fly.dev
- API server: add production URL to OpenAPI servers list
- CLI/SDK READMEs: update all URL references

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(provisioner): per-database Fly app isolation (US-002)

Each database now gets its own Fly app (snoopgres-db-{name}) with a unique
hostname and reliable TCP routing on port 5432. The provisioner creates the
app before the machine, uses handlers: ['tls'] for SSL termination, and
cleans up the app on destroy. All callers updated to pass flyAppName
explicitly instead of using FLY_APP_NAME env var.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(tls): add TLS/SSL support for Postgres connections (US-004)

- Connection strings now include ?sslmode=require for Fly TLS proxy
- Migration runner uses ssl: { rejectUnauthorized: false } for Fly certs
- CLI connect command ensures sslmode=require is always present
- Snapshot dumper exec API calls are unaffected (run inside machine)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(sdk+mcp): update SDK, MCP tools, and types for per-app architecture (US-007)

Add fly_app_name field to DatabaseInfo, DatabaseSummary, CreateDatabaseResponse,
and Database shared types. Update API routes to return fly_app_name and hostname
in responses. Update MCP tool schemas and descriptions to reflect TLS-enabled
connection strings and per-app hostnames. Update SDK README with new connection
string format (snoopgres-db-{name}.fly.dev with sslmode=require) and TLS pg
pool example.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(cli): update CLI commands for per-app architecture and TLS (US-005)

- connstring: ensure sslmode=require in output for TLS connections
- list: add HOSTNAME column showing per-app fly.dev hostnames
- info: display fly_app_name and hostname fields alongside existing details
- destroy: warn that associated Fly app will be deleted in confirmation
- connect/api-client: already updated in US-003/US-004 (no changes needed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(admin): add migration tool for per-database Fly app isolation (US-006)

Add `snoopgres admin migrate-isolation` CLI command and API endpoints
to migrate existing databases from the shared snoopgres-db-engine app
into individual per-database Fly apps. Supports --dry-run preview,
progress output, and rollback safety (old machines stopped, not deleted).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(e2e): add fly-isolation architecture e2e tests (US-008)

Add comprehensive e2e tests verifying per-database Fly app isolation:
- Offline tests: CLI help/format, API URL config, connection string structure
- Live tests (gated by SNOOPGRES_E2E_API_KEY): full create->query->destroy
  lifecycle verifying per-app hostnames, TLS connections, SQL operations
- Update coverage.json with new spec (66 total tests)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* e2e: post-project guardian audit -- update journeys and tests for snoopgres-fly-isolation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Fix drawio export: git add *.svg and fix doc references (#27)

The export action with output: . and remove-page-suffix: true produces
*.svg files (not *.drawio.svg). Fix git add glob to match, and update
README and architecture.md references accordingly.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove redundant preferred_model, add config fingerprint for upgrade propagation

Remove the preferred_model / model_override fields that duplicated the
per-provider model selector. The per-provider model field is now the single
source of truth for model configuration.

Add a config fingerprint (sha256 of manifest-affecting broker config fields)
stored per instance. The upgrade-agents errand now triggers redeployment
when either the OpenClaw version OR the config fingerprint changes, ensuring
tile-level config changes (model swaps, adapter changes, etc.) propagate to
existing instances automatically.
